machine.name: Home Chronic Pain Monitor
machine.description: Monitors pain intensity, functional impairment, opioid risk indicators, and physical activity for adults managing chronic pain at home in low- and medium-income settings. Chronic pain is disproportionately prevalent in low-income populations due to occupational injury, inadequate early treatment, and limited access to non-pharmacological interventions. Unmanaged chronic pain is a primary driver of depression and social isolation. Output at [2023:2027] feeds the Home Mental Health Access Monitor alongside the Chronic Disease Monitor.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Adults with chronic pain from occupational injury, musculoskeletal conditions, fibromyalgia; opioid-prescribed patients in home care; low-income adults with limited PT/pain clinic access

sensorNormalization.pain_scale_norm: 0.0=pain 9–10/10 constant, unable to function, 0.5=pain 4–6/10 manageable with medication, 1.0=pain 0–2/10 minimal or absent
sensorNormalization.functional_impairment_norm: 0.0=bedbound or homebound due to pain; unable to perform basic ADLs, 0.5=significantly limited but managing key daily activities with help, 1.0=fully functional with minimal pain interference
sensorNormalization.opioid_risk_norm: 0.0=multiple opioid risk flags (early refills, sedation signs, diversion indicators, dose escalation without consult), 0.5=one risk factor present (borderline early refill; PRN overuse), 1.0=no opioid risk indicators; stable prescribed use or non-opioid pain management
sensorNormalization.physical_activity_norm: 0.0=zero physical activity; complete deconditioning, 0.5=minimal movement with assistance; ADL-level activity only, 1.0=regular therapeutic activity; walking program or PT adherent

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning HomeChronicPainMonitor matures.

rules.description: PAIN_CRISIS: uncontrolled acute pain episode requiring urgent pain management intervention at [2023:2027]
OPIOID_RISK_ELEVATED: opioid misuse or overuse pattern detected at [2023:2027]
FUNCTIONAL_IMPAIRMENT: chronic pain causing sustained ADL and activity limitation at [2023:2027]
PAIN_MANAGED: chronic pain adequately controlled with maintained function at [2023:2027]

sequences.name: Pain Crisis: MANAGED+ → WORSENING → SEVERE → PAIN_CRISIS

metadata.description: Tracks progressive pain control failure toward functional crisis. In low-income home care, pain crisis episodes are often precipitated by medication access failures (opioid prior auth denied, pharmacy unable to fill, cost), weather-related flares, or loss of PT access. The MANAGED+ baseline confirms prior pain control before escalation can signal.

metadata.name: MANAGED
metadata.description: MANAGED+ — pain 3–5/10 manageable; functional for essential ADLs; opioid use stable; some physical activity maintained. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: WORSENING
metadata.description: WORSENING — pain escalating to 6–7/10; functional limitations increasing; activity declining; PRN medication use increasing.

metadata.name: SEVERE
metadata.description: SEVERE — pain 8–10/10; ADL-dependent; activity ceased; medication inadequate. Fires PAIN_CRISIS.
metadata.pain_scale: 0.0–0.18 (pain 8–10/10; crisis requiring urgent pain management intervention)

metadata.description: PAIN_CRISIS: uncontrolled acute pain episode requiring urgent pain management intervention at [2023:2027]
metadata.action: Urgent nurse visit; physician pain consult; medication adjustment; crisis pain plan activation

metadata.description: Tracks opioid risk escalation patterns — early refill requests, dose escalation without physician contact, sedation signs — in home care patients on prescribed opioids. Low-income patients are at higher opioid misuse risk due to untreated pain, mental health comorbidities, and limited alternative pain management access.

metadata.name: STABLE_RX
metadata.description: STABLE_RX+ — opioid prescription adherent; no early refill requests; function adequate; no sedation signs. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: RISK_SIGNAL
metadata.description: RISK_SIGNAL — one or more opioid risk flags present: early refill request; reported sedation; pain score escalating despite stable rx. Fires OPIOID_RISK_ELEVATED.
metadata.opioid_risk: 0.28–0.45 (one or more ORT risk factors active)

metadata.description: OPIOID_RISK_ELEVATED: opioid misuse or overuse pattern detected at [2023:2027]
metadata.action: ORT/DIRE risk re-screening; physician medication review; urine drug screen; PDMP check; non-opioid alternative assessment

metadata.description: Fires FUNCTIONAL_IMPAIRMENT when pain causes persistent ADL limitation without reaching crisis level. The largest chronic pain population segment — patients managing at home with significant daily function loss but not acute enough for emergency escalation. Drives depression, social withdrawal, and caregiver burden.

metadata.name: FUNCTIONALLY_IMPAIRED
metadata.description: FUNCTIONALLY_IMPAIRED+ — pain limiting major functional activities; opioid use stable but inadequate; minimal physical activity. isInitial A*A = A+: fires FUNCTIONAL_IMPAIRMENT each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Back injury patient; pain 6/10 daily; cannot work; on long-term opioid rx; no PT access due to transport/cost

metadata.description: FUNCTIONAL_IMPAIRMENT: chronic pain causing sustained ADL and activity limitation at [2023:2027]
metadata.action: PT referral; pain management plan review; occupational therapy for adaptive function; depression screening

metadata.description: Fires PAIN_MANAGED when pain is adequately controlled, function maintained, opioid use stable, and activity preserved. In home care this reflects successful multimodal pain management — often the outcome of community health worker support combined with medication management.

metadata.name: PAIN_CONTROLLED
metadata.description: PAIN_CONTROLLED+ — pain ≤4/10 with current regimen; functional for most ADLs; opioid use stable with no risk flags; maintaining therapeutic activity. Fires PAIN_MANAGED each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Patient on stable opioid + NSAID combination; enrolled in home PT program; CHW support for activity

metadata.description: PAIN_MANAGED: chronic pain adequately controlled with maintained function at [2023:2027]
metadata.action: Mental health access monitor registers pain management stable

inputSequences.name: Winter Flare with Prior Auth Denial
inputSequences.description: Patient's prior authorization for extended-release opioid denied by insurer; winter cold worsens back pain; flare to crisis; early short-acting use flags risk; pain management review resolves.
